prompt:
You are building a document formatter. Write a function `title_case_format(text)` that converts a string into proper title case for headings.

Rules:
1. The first letter of each word should be uppercase, the rest lowercase.
2. However, certain small words should remain entirely lowercase UNLESS they are the first word of the title.
3. The small words are: "a", "an", "the", "of", "in", "on", "at", "to", "for", "and", "but", "or"

Words in the input are separated by single spaces. The input may contain any mix of uppercase and lowercase letters.

Examples:
  title_case_format("the lord of the rings") => "The Lord of the Rings"
  title_case_format("WAR AND PEACE") => "War and Peace"
  title_case_format("a tale of two cities") => "A Tale of Two Cities"
  title_case_format("") => ""

Input: A string of space-separated words.
Output: The string converted to title case following the rules above.
validation_trace: Traced reference solution against all 10 tests: (1) 'the lord of the rings' -> 'The Lord of the Rings' ✓; (2) 'WAR AND PEACE' -> 'War and Peace' ✓; (3) 'a tale of two cities' -> 'A Tale of Two Cities' ✓; (4) '' -> '' ✓; (5) 'hello' -> 'Hello' ✓; (6) 'the' -> 'The' ✓; (7) 'in the beginning' -> 'In the Beginning' ✓; (8) 'pRiDe AND pReJuDiCe' -> 'Pride and Prejudice' ✓; (9) 'GREAT BIG WORLD' -> 'Great Big World' ✓; (10) 'an ode to the art of living' -> 'An Ode to the Art of Living' ✓. All pass.
